title: Almanac dataset catalog entry
description: One curated entry describing a public dataset: what it is, where it canonically lives, how to access it, where it is archived, and whether it is still reachable. The catalog maps data; it does not host it.

id.description: Stable kebab-case slug. Must match the filename (minus .yaml). Never reused.

description.description: One or two sentences: what the dataset is and why it matters.

publisher.description: Authoritative producer — the agency or institution that publishes the data.

topics.description: Free-tag topics for discovery, e.g. [temperature, sea-ice] or [mortality, surveillance, weekly].

canonical_url.description: Current authoritative home of the data.

predecessor_url.description: A prior or decommissioned location where this data used to be surfaced, if known. The preservation trail. Null if N/A.

method.description: How the data is actually retrieved.

auth_note.description: What credential is needed, e.g. 'free API token'.

format.description: File/serialization formats offered, e.g. [csv, json, netcdf, geotiff, parquet].

spatial.description: e.g. global, national, a named region.

archive.description: Where copies live, beyond the canonical source.

cloud_mirror.description: Open-data cloud mirror path (e.g. an AWS Open Data S3 path), if mirrored there.

mirror.description: Populated ONLY if this project hosts a copy.

status.description: live=reachable & maintained; moved=URL changed; dark=gone/404; frozen=reachable but no longer updated; mirrored=we hold a copy.

last_checked.description: ISO date the status was last verified (YYYY-MM-DD). The reachability checker informs this.

checksum.description: Checksum of a mirrored artifact, if archive.mirror is set.

notes.description: Curator notes: caveats, history, why status changed.
